chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA Constt: Petition.No.D-19 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 01. For orders on office objection-A’. 02. For orders on M.A.No.190/2026. (E/A) 03. For hearing of main case. 27.01.2026 Mr. Muhammad Afzal Jagirani, Advocate for the petitioner. -------------- The petitioner seeks appointment as civil servant on the deceased quota under Rule 11-A of the Sindh Civil Servants (Appointment, Promotion & Transfer) Rules, 1974. However, Rule 11-A has since been declared unconstitutional by the Hon'ble Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of General Post Office, Islamabad v. Muhammad Jalal (PLD 2024 SC- 1276). Thereafter, Rule 11-A was also omitted. Learned counsel submits that the petitioner's application for appointment on the deceased quota was filed before the case of Muhammad Jalal. Even so, pending applications for appointment under Rule11-A were not protected by the case of Muhammad Jalal. That aspect has already been discussed by this Court in the Referee opinion dated 24.11.2025 in the case of Sanaullah v. Deputy Commissioner, Larkana (C.P.No.D-99/2025). Therefore, in view of the foregoing, the petition is not maintainable. Same is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE S.Ashfaq/
